dimensions.name: routing_precision
dimensions.description: The response chooses native, research, debug, plan, execute, review, goal, init, or update only when the task evidence warrants it.

dimensions.name: scope_control
dimensions.description: The response honors owned paths, protected boundaries, platform limits, and accepted-plan constraints.

dimensions.name: evidence_quality
dimensions.description: Claims are backed by observed files, command output, source lookup, or clearly labeled inference.

dimensions.name: user_burden
dimensions.description: The response avoids unnecessary plans, questions, artifacts, gates, and subagent dispatch for lightweight work.

dimensions.name: plan_readiness
dimensions.description: A proposed plan maps requirements to owned actions and proof, sources execution-critical values, preserves unresolved blockers, and is executable without reopening design.

dimensions.name: plan_grill_authority
dimensions.description: Non-simple planning resolves evidence first, asks only material user-owned decisions one at a time, confirms a Decision Summary before the final plan, and keeps implementation authority separate from plan confirmation.

dimensions.name: convergence_monotonicity
dimensions.description: Reviews preserve stable identities, distinguish direct regressions from unrelated findings and pre-existing follow-ups, never accept with blockers, version contracts only for accepted scope deltas, and retry only the affected goal stage.

dimensions.name: release_governance
dimensions.description: Release claims rely on non-empty release evals, validation evidence, ledgers, and fresh review when required.
